title: Intense Korean Mushroom Stew
description: A cultured, velvety Korean stew with bold flavors from gochujang and soy sauce, finished with a gentle flambé of sesame oil.

ingredients.name: dried shiitake mushrooms
ingredients.notes: reconstitute in 200 ml hot water for 30 min then slice

ingredients.name: dried oyster mushrooms
ingredients.notes: reconstitute in 200 ml hot water for 30 min then slice

instructions: Rehydrate dried shiitake and oyster mushrooms by soaking them in 200 ml hot water for 30 minutes, then slice into bite-size pieces.
Heat sesame oil in a pot over medium heat; add sliced garlic and ginger, sauté until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Stir in the sliced mushrooms, coating them with oil and aromatics, cook for 5 minutes until they start to soften.
Add gochujang, soy sauce, and a splash of vegetable broth, stirring to combine the sauce.
Pour in the remaining vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 30 minutes, uncovered.
During the last 10 minutes, add scallions, seaweed flakes, toasted sesame seeds, and black pepper, stirring to integrate the flavors.
Once the stew has thickened to a velvety consistency, taste and adjust seasoning with extra soy sauce if needed.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with a few extra scallion leaves for a fresh finish.
